unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: Welcome to your first unit of Year 2 English! In this unit you will learn how to introduce yourself and your friends, talk about what people like, and use the calendar — the days of the week, the months of the year, and dates. You will also notice English words all around you and ask simple questions about colours and numbers. By the end, you will be ready to welcome a new friend, just like Sarah does in this unit's story.
unit.learningPath:
Start with the Vocabulary file. Learn a few words each day.
Do the three lessons in the Grammar file (he/she, like/likes, and questions).
Read the Story file, “Sarah's First Week”, and answer the questions.
Work through the four skills below: Reading & Listening → Speaking → Writing → Activities.
Finish with the Self-Assessment to see what you have learned.

visual.alt: Children greeting one another beside a colourful classroom calendar

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The word that a person is called.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My name is Samira. What is your name?
practiceSentences: My name is Samira. What is your name?
We learned the word name in our greetings and introductions lesson.
I wrote name in my vocabulary book.
Can you find or show name in the picture?
I can use name in a clear sentence.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: n - a - m - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: Use the word 'name' in a sentence, then explain what it means.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: A person you work or play with.

dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: Use the word 'partner' in a sentence, then explain what it means.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To enjoy something or think it is good.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: I like mangoes because they are sweet.
practiceSentences: I like mangoes because they are sweet.

We like together when it is our turn.
Please like safely and carefully.
Can you like and explain what you did?
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: l - i - k - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: Use the word 'like' in a sentence, then explain what it means.

I heard twenty-third in our number game.

outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Demonstrated through the mapped reading, speaking, writing, activity, quiz or live-session evidence in this unit.

outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Demonstrated through the mapped reading, speaking, writing, activity, quiz or live-session evidence in this unit.

outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Demonstrated through the mapped reading, speaking, writing, activity, quiz or live-session evidence in this unit.

outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Demonstrated through the mapped reading, speaking, writing, activity, quiz or live-session evidence in this unit.
Demonstrated through the mapped reading, speaking, writing, activity, quiz or live-session evidence in this unit.

readings.title: Words Around Us
readings.passageScript:
Read this short text:
Words are all around us. We can see words in books. We can see words on tablets. We can see words on signs. We can see words at home. Words help us learn!

readings.title: A Poem: “When I Open Up a Book”
readings.passageScript:
When I open up a book
Each page whispers, “Look! Look! Look!”
Sports and monkeys, trains and kings,
Stories of amazing things.

readings.title: Meeting a Partner
readings.passageScript:
Listen to Amara introduce her partner. (Ask your AI tutor to read it aloud, then read it yourself.)
“This is my partner. Her name is Hannah. She likes trees. She is my friend.”

readings.title: The Days of the Week Song
readings.passageScript:
Sing along:
“Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday. Seven days make one week — seven days in all!”

readings.title: Sarah's First Week
readings.theme: Meeting new friends, saying your name, and the days of the week.
readings.setting: A primary school in a Somali town.
readings.passageScript:
It was Sarah's first day at a new school. The school was near the market in her town. Sarah held her mother's hand and looked at the big blue door.
“Good morning,” said a kind teacher. “Welcome! My name is Teacher Nadia. What is your name?”
“My name is Sarah,” she said in a small voice.
“Can you spell it for me, Sarah?” asked Teacher Nadia.
“N-A-S-R-A,” said Sarah. She felt a little braver now.
Inside the classroom, the children sat in a circle. On the wall there was a big calendar and a chart of colours. A boy with a yellow cap smiled at Sarah.
“Hello! My name is Adam,” he said. “Today is Saturday. It is the first day of our school week. You can be my partner!”
“Thank you, Adam,” said Sarah. She liked him already.
Teacher Nadia pointed to the calendar. “Let us say the days of the week together,” she said. The whole class sang: “Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday!”
“Now,” said Teacher Nadia, “tell your partner one thing you like.”
Hannah turned to Adam. “I like drawing,” she said. “What do you like?”
“I like football,” said Adam. “And I like mangoes! My birthday is in the month of June, when the mangoes are sweet.”
Hannah laughed. “My birthday is in December,” she said. “I will be eight years old.”
All week, Sarah learned something new. On Monday she learned her colours. On Tuesday she counted to twelve. On Wednesday she read her first English book about a clever fox.
On Thursday, a new girl came to the class. She stood by the door and looked shy, just like Sarah on her first day.
Hannah remembered how kind Adam had been. She walked over and smiled.
“Hello,” said Sarah. “My name is Sarah. What is your name? You can be my partner today.”
The new girl smiled back. “My name is Amal,” she said. “Thank you.”
Teacher Nadia watched and nodded. “Well done, Sarah,” she said softly. “A good friend makes everyone feel welcome.”
That night, Sarah told her mother about her week. “I have two friends now,” she said happily. “Adam and Amal. And I know all the days of the week!”
Her mother hugged her. “You had a wonderful first week,” she said. “Tomorrow is Friday — a day to rest.”
Hannah closed her eyes and smiled. She could not wait for Saturday, when a brand new school week would begin again.

comprehension.question: Where can you see words? List every place in the text.
comprehension.correctAnswer: Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: We can see words in books. We can see words on tablets. We can see words on signs. We can see words at home. Words help us learn!
comprehension.explanation: The response should point to this evidence from the text: We can see words in books. We can see words on tablets. We can see words on signs. We can see words at home. Words help us learn!

comprehension.question: Where else can you see words that the text does not mention?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

comprehension.question: Look around your own home. Where do you see words?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

comprehension.question: What happens when you open a book, in the poem?
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What do you like to read about? (You will write your own poem later.)
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

comprehension.question: Who is Amara's partner?  What does Hannah like?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Amara's partner is Hannah, and Hannah likes trees.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

comprehension.question: Does Amara say “he” or “she” about Hannah? Why? (See Grammar Lesson 1.)
comprehension.correctAnswer: Amara says ‘she’ because Hannah is a girl; the text also uses ‘her’ for Hannah.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: How many days are in a week?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Seven days make one week — seven days in all!”
comprehension.explanation: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Seven days make one week — seven days in all!”

comprehension.question: What is the first day of your school week?  What day comes after Wednesday?
comprehension.correctAnswer: The first school day depends on the learner's school. Thursday comes after Wednesday.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What is the name of the new girl on the first day?
comprehension.correctAnswer: The new girl on the first day is Sarah.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: Who is Sarah's first partner? What does he like?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Sarah's first partner is Adam. He likes football and mangoes.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What is the first day of the school week in the story?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Saturday is the first day of the school week in the story.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: In which month is Adam's birthday? In which month is Sarah's?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Adam's birthday is in June. Sarah's birthday is in December.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: What kind thing did Sarah do for Amal on Thursday? Why?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Sarah welcomed Amal and offered to be her partner because she remembered Adam's kindness on her own first day.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: How did Sarah feel on her first day? How did she feel by Thursday? What changed?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Sarah felt shy and nervous at first. By Thursday she felt brave and confident enough to welcome Amal. Her new friendships helped her change.
comprehension.explanation: Curriculum review correction: answer aligned directly to the approved passage.

comprehension.question: Teacher Nadia said, “A good friend makes everyone feel welcome.” How do you make a new person feel welcome?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

comprehension.question: How was Sarah's week the same as, or different from, your own week at school?
comprehension.correctAnswer: Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
comprehension.explanation: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

grammar.title: He and She: Choose the Pronoun
grammar.explanation: We use he and she to talk about one person without saying the person's name again. Use he for a boy or man and she for a girl or woman.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
This is Noah. He likes football.
This is Selma. She likes reading.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: This is my sister. He likes drawing.
Fix: This is my sister. She likes drawing.
grammar.memoryTip: She and sister both begin with the sound sh.
grammar.practice: Choose he or she: This is my brother. ___ likes running. | This is Samira. ___ likes singing. | This is my father. ___ works in the market. | This is Sarah. ___ is my best friend.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: He and She: Introduce a Person
grammar.explanation: Now use he or she in your own introduction. Say the person's name first, then use the correct pronoun in the next sentence.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
This is Adam. He is my partner. He likes football.
This is Amal. She is my friend. She likes books.
grammar.commonMistake: Do not change between he and she when you are still talking about the same person.
grammar.practice: Choose one boy or man and one girl or woman. Write or say two sentences about each person using he or she.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Like and Likes: Choose the Form
grammar.explanation: Use like with I, you, we and they. Use likes with he, she and it.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
I like bananas. We like singing.
He likes football. She likes drawing.
grammar.commonMistake:
Mistake: She like mangoes.
Fix: She likes mangoes.
grammar.memoryTip: He, she, it: add s to likes.
grammar.practice: Choose like or likes: I ___ football. | My mother ___ tea. | They ___ the calendar song. | He ___ funny stories. | We ___ our new school.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Like and Likes: Class Survey
grammar.explanation: Ask a person what they like. Report the answer using likes when you talk about one other person.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
What do you like? I like drawing.
Samira likes drawing.
What does Adam like? He likes football.
grammar.commonMistake: Use like in the answer I like, but use likes when reporting he or she likes.
grammar.practice: Ask two people what they like. Record or write each answer. Then report: ___ likes ___.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Asking Questions: Build It
grammar.explanation: Use clear question patterns to ask about spelling, colours and numbers.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
How do you spell your name?
What colour is your bag? It is blue.
How many chairs are there? There are six chairs.
grammar.commonMistake: After how many, most countable nouns need s: How many chairs are there?
grammar.memoryTip: Question word first, question mark last.
grammar.practice: Build a question for each answer: My name is spelled A-M-I-N-A. | My bag is green. | There are twelve months.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

grammar.title: Asking Questions: Ask and Answer
grammar.explanation: Practise asking a complete question and answering it in a complete sentence.
grammar.ruleAndExamples:
What colour is the calendar? The calendar is blue.
How many books are there? There are four books.
grammar.commonMistake: An answer should match the question. A colour question needs a colour answer.
grammar.memoryTip: Listen for the question word before you answer.
grammar.practice: Ask and answer six questions: two about spelling, two about colours and two about numbers.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

speaking.title: Speaking 1 — Introduce Yourself
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Get ready to say:
“My name is ______.”
“I am ______ years old.”
“I like ______.”
Record yourself. Check: Did you say your name clearly? Did you say what you like? Did you speak loudly enough?

speaking.title: Speaking 2 — Talk About Your Birthday
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
“My birthday is on the ______ of ______.”
“On my birthday, I like to ______.”

speaking.title: Speaking Game — I Spy
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines:
Play with your AI tutor or your family:
Choose an object you can see. Say: “I spy something ______ (colour).”
The other person guesses: “Is it the ______?”
Answer: “Yes, it is!” or “No, it isn't.” Then swap turns.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Spell Your Name
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Ask: What is your name? How do you spell it? Answer: My name is Samira. I spell it A-M-I-N-A. Swap roles and speak clearly.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my name and spelling. Tell me whether every letter is clear.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Calendar Chant
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Say the seven days in order. Then say the twelve months in order. Start slowly, repeat at a steady pace, and finish by saying today's date.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my calendar chant and tell me if any day or month is missing or out of order.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - Welcome a New Friend
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Role-play meeting a new classmate. Say: Hello. My name is ___. What is your name? You can be my partner. Ask what the person likes and respond kindly.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Role-play a new classmate. Answer my questions and give me feedback on my welcome.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

writing.title: Writing 1 — Simple Sentences About You
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Finish these sentences, then write two more of your own:
My name is ______.
I like ______.
My friend's name is ______.
He / She likes ______.
writing.modelText: My name is Samira. I like reading. My friend's name is Selma. She likes drawing.
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Writing 2 — Your Own Poem
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Write your own version of “When I Open Up a Book”. Change the third line to things you like to read about:
When I open up a book / Each page whispers, “Look! Look! Look!” / ______ and ______, ______ and ______, / Stories of amazing things.
Then draw a picture to go with your poem.
writing.modelText:
When I open up a book,
Each page whispers, Look, look, look!
Trains and planets, birds that sing,
Stories show me everything.
writing.sentenceStarter: When I open up a book,
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Story response: Sarah's First Week
writing.promptAndInstructions:
Write three sentences about your own first week at school. Start with: “On Monday I ______.”
Draw Sarah, Adam and Amal. Write one sentence under your picture about what each person likes.
writing.modelText: On Monday I felt shy at school. A kind friend welcomed me. By the end of the week, I felt happy and brave.
writing.sentenceStarter: On Monday I felt shy at school.
writing.successCriteria: I answered the prompt; used Unit vocabulary; wrote complete sentences; used capital letters, spaces and end punctuation; reread my work.
writing.support: Use the sentence starter, word bank and model. Say each sentence aloud before writing.
writing.extension: Add two precise details, a joining word and one sentence explaining why.

writing.title: Writing 4 - My Special Date
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write four sentences about a special date. Include the day, date, month and why it is special.
writing.modelText: My special date is Saturday, the 3rd of August. It is my birthday. My family shares a meal. I feel happy on this day.
writing.successCriteria: I named a day and month; wrote the date correctly; explained why it is special; used capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use the calendar word bank and the model date.
writing.extension: Add a fifth sentence using because.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

writing.title: Writing 5 - Welcome Card
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write a welcome card for a new student. Introduce yourself, say what you like, ask one question and offer help.
writing.modelText: Hello Amal! My name is Sarah. I like drawing. What do you like? You can sit with me today. Welcome to our class!
writing.successCriteria: I greeted the student; introduced myself; asked a question; used kind words; checked punctuation
writing.support: Use hello, name, like, partner and welcome.
writing.extension: Add a helpful detail about the classroom or timetable.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

writing.title: Writing 6 - My Two-Week Learning Diary
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write one sentence about something you learned in each of the six live sessions. Finish with one goal for your next English unit.
writing.modelText: In Session 1, I introduced myself. In Session 2, I read about Sarah. I can now say the months in order. Next, I want to read more smoothly.
writing.successCriteria: I reflected on all six sessions; used complete sentences; included one learning goal; reread my work
writing.support: Look at your saved work and session titles before writing.
writing.extension: Explain which task helped you most and why.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

activities.title: Activity 1 — True or False (Calendar Facts)
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Write True or False. If it is false, write the correct answer.
There are 12 months in a year.
There are 8 days in a week.
January is the first month of the year.
September has 31 days.
activities.answerSummary: 1. True  2. False (7 days)  3. True  4. False (30 days)

activities.title: Activity 2 — Match the Months to the Season
activities.instructionsAndItems: Where you live, which months are hot and dry, and which months have rain? Make two lists and check with your AI tutor, because seasons are different in different places.

activities.title: Activity 3 — Fill in the Blanks
activities.instructionsAndItems:
Today is ______ (day of the week).
My birthday is in the month of ______.
The first day of my school week is ______.
There are ______ months in a year.

activities.title: Activity 4 - Put the Days in Order
activities.instructionsAndItems: Put these days in the school-week order used in the story: Wednesday | Saturday | Monday | Friday | Tuesday | Thursday | Sunday.
activities.answerSummary: Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday
activities.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

activities.title: Activity 5 - Month Detective
activities.instructionsAndItems: Find and record: the first month; the sixth month; the month after August; the month before December; your birthday month.
activities.answerSummary: January; June; September; November; learner response
activities.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

activities.title: Activity 6 - Colour and Number Hunt
activities.instructionsAndItems: Find three objects. Ask and answer: What colour is it? How many can you see? Record each answer in a complete sentence.
activities.answerSummary: Answers vary; require three relevant colour-and-number sentences.
activities.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

assignments.title: Welcome and Calendar learning portfolio
assignments.instructions: Complete 'Writing 1 — Simple Sentences About You' and record 'Speaking 1 — Introduce Yourself'. Submit both pieces after checking the writing and speaking rubrics.
assignments.sourceFile: Derived from unit writing and speaking tasks

quizzes.question: What does 'name' mean?
quizzes.options: The word that a person is called. | A person you work or play with. | To enjoy something or think it is good. | To say or write the letters of a word in the right order.
quizzes.correctAnswer: The word that a person is called.
quizzes.explanation: My name is Samira. What is your name?

quizzes.question: Which word means: A person you work or play with.
quizzes.explanation: Ishmael is my partner for the reading game.

quizzes.question: What type of word is 'like'?
quizzes.explanation: I like mangoes because they are sweet.

quizzes.question: Can you ____ your name for me, please?
quizzes.explanation: Can you spell your name for me, please?

quizzes.question: Which spelling is correct?
quizzes.explanation: My friend Selma sits next to me.

quizzes.question: Which day comes after Wednesday?
quizzes.explanation: The order is Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: How many months are in one year?
quizzes.explanation: A calendar year has twelve months.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: This is Samira. ___ likes reading.
quizzes.explanation: Use she when talking about one girl or woman.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: Adam ___ football.
quizzes.explanation: Use likes with he, she or one named person.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

quizzes.question: Which is a complete colour question?
quizzes.options: What colour is your bag? | Your bag colour. | How colour bag? | Is colour your bag.
quizzes.explanation: The complete pattern is: What colour is ...?
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Welcome, introductions and vocabulary
liveSessions.beforeSession: Watch the teacher lecture and learn the first greeting words.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min welcome; 10 min name-and-spelling circle; 10 min vocabulary retrieval; 15 min partner introductions; 5 min reflection
liveSessions.afterSession: Record Speaking 1 and add five words to My Word Book.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Shared reading: Sarah's First Week
liveSessions.beforeSession: Listen to Sarah's First Week and bring one question.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min retrieval; 10 min teacher read-aloud; 10 min echo reading; 15 min story questions; 5 min welcome-message exit ticket
liveSessions.afterSession: Improve two comprehension answers using story evidence.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Pronouns, likes and speaking
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min warm-up; 10 min he/she sorting; 10 min like/likes mini-lesson; 15 min class survey; 5 min feedback
liveSessions.afterSession: Record Speaking 4 and correct one grammar mistake.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Calendar language and questions
liveSessions.beforeSession: Practise the days, months and today's date.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min calendar chant; 10 min sequencing challenge; 10 min asking questions; 15 min colour-and-number hunt; 5 min review
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Guided writing and feedback
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring a draft of Writing 4 or Writing 5.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min model review; 10 min sentence rehearsal; 15 min guided writing; 10 min teacher or peer feedback; 5 min revision goal
liveSessions.afterSession: Revise and submit one writing piece to the portfolio.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

liveSessions.title: Review, quiz and portfolio celebration
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete the ten-question quiz and choose one piece to share.
liveSessions.agenda: 10 min retrieval game; 10 min quiz corrections; 15 min speaking and writing shares; 5 min self-assessment; 5 min next-unit goal
liveSessions.afterSession: Complete Writing 6 and save the final self-assessment.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

teacherNotes.note:
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you read the poem with me and explain it?” “Can you play a listening game about the days of the week?”
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you help me practise introducing myself?” “Can you play ‘I Spy’ with me?” “How do I say the date of my birthday correctly?”
Lesson: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you check if my sentences are correct?” “Can you help me make my poem rhyme?”
Story: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you read the story with me and explain any hard words?” “Can you check my sentences about my school week?” “Can you ask me questions about the story to see if I understood?”
Grammar: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you check my grammar answers?” “Why do we say ‘he likes’ but ‘I like’?” “Can you give me five more sentences to practise he and she?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you help me practise introducing myself?” “How do I spell this word: ______?” “Can you give me other ways to say what I like?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you quiz me on the days of the week and the months?” “How do I say today's date correctly?” “Which months are hot and which months have rain where I live?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you help me read this word I found: ______?” “What does this English word mean?”
Vocabulary: 💬 Ask Your AI Tutor “Can you quiz me on colour words?” “Can you help me count to twelve in English?”

Term placement approved. Outcome mappings, comprehension guidance, vocabulary enrichment, assessments, live sessions, rubrics, writing specifications and answer-key coverage completed in the approved reference model.
teacherNotes.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1 migration

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: 1. True  2. False (7 days)  3. True  4. False (30 days)
1. He   2. She   3. He   4. She
1. like   2. likes   3. like   4. likes   5. like

How do you spell your name?
What colour is your bag?
How many months are there? (in a year)

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: We can see words in books. We can see words on tablets. We can see words on signs. We can see words at home. Words help us learn!
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: We can see words in books. We can see words on tablets. We can see words on signs. We can see words at home. Words help us learn!

Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
Marking guidance: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
Marking guidance: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: When I open up a book Each page whispers, “Look!
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: When I open up a book Each page whispers, “Look!

Answers will vary. Accept a clear, relevant response in a complete sentence with a reason, example or text detail where appropriate.
Marking guidance: Award full credit when the learner answers every part of the prompt and supports the response with a sensible detail.

Amara's partner is Hannah, and Hannah likes trees.
Marking guidance: Award full credit for this answer or an equivalent response that communicates the same meaning.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Her name is Hannah.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Her name is Hannah.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Seven days make one week — seven days in all!”
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Seven days make one week — seven days in all!”

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: Seven days make one week — seven days in all!”
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: Seven days make one week — seven days in all!”

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: It was Sarah's first day at a new school.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: It was Sarah's first day at a new school.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: The whole class sang: “Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday!” “Now,” said Teacher Nadia, “tell your partner one thing you like.” Sarah turned to Adam. She stood by the door and looked shy, just like Sarah on her first day.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: The whole class sang: “Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday!” “Now,” said Teacher Nadia, “tell your partner one thing you like.” Sarah turned to Adam. She stood by the door and looked shy, just like Sarah on her first day.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: It is the first day of our school week.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: It is the first day of our school week.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: My birthday is in the month of June, when the mangoes are sweet.” Sarah laughed. You can be my partner!” “Thank you, Adam,” said Sarah.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: My birthday is in the month of June, when the mangoes are sweet.” Sarah laughed. You can be my partner!” “Thank you, Adam,” said Sarah.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: The whole class sang: “Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday!” “Now,” said Teacher Nadia, “tell your partner one thing you like.” Sarah turned to Adam. Sarah remembered how kind Adam had been.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: The whole class sang: “Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday!” “Now,” said Teacher Nadia, “tell your partner one thing you like.” Sarah turned to Adam. Sarah remembered how kind Adam had been.

Accept an answer that communicates this evidence: It was Sarah's first day at a new school. She stood by the door and looked shy, just like Sarah on her first day.
Marking guidance: The response should point to this evidence from the text: It was Sarah's first day at a new school. She stood by the door and looked shy, just like Sarah on her first day.

Correct answer: The word that a person is called.. My name is Samira. What is your name?
Correct answer: partner. Ishmael is my partner for the reading game.
Correct answer: Verb. I like mangoes because they are sweet.
Correct answer: spell. Can you spell your name for me, please?
Correct answer: friend. My friend Selma sits next to me.
Assess the writing with rub-writing-v1 and the recording with rub-speaking-v1. Award up to 16 marks for each artifact.

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Choose one boy or man and one girl or woman. Write or say two sentences about each person using he or she.
answerKey.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Ask two people what they like. Record or write each answer. Then report: ___ likes ___.
answerKey.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Ask and answer six questions: two about spelling, two about colours and two about numbers.
answerKey.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Use the speaking rubric. Accept clear, relevant oral language that follows the model and fulfils every instruction.

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Use the writing rubric and this approved model: My special date is Saturday, the 3rd of August. It is my birthday. My family shares a meal. I feel happy on this day.
answerKey.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Use the writing rubric and this approved model: Hello Amal! My name is Sarah. I like drawing. What do you like? You can sit with me today. Welcome to our class!
answerKey.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Use the writing rubric and this approved model: In Session 1, I introduced myself. In Session 2, I read about Sarah. I can now say the months in order. Next, I want to read more smoothly.
answerKey.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Saturday, Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: January; June; September; November; learner response

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Answers vary; require three relevant colour-and-number sentences.

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: Thursday. The order is Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday.

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: 12. A calendar year has twelve months.

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: She. Use she when talking about one girl or woman.
answerKey.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 curriculum expansion

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: likes. Use likes with he, she or one named person.

answerKey.answerOrGuidance: What colour is your bag?. The complete pattern is: What colour is ...?
